commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because the blockers are security design approval, draft state, and real iOS behavior proof rather than a narrow safe repair.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete credential-exposure concern by putting shared gateway secrets into setup-code payloads.; Review finding: [P1] Keep setup codes off shared gateway secrets — src/pairing/setup-code.ts:376-384 - [P2] Keep QR docs consistent... (sha=cc718dee4f7a308efb6b71b8864ec4f574e0647b)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes setup-code generation from bootstrap tokens to shared token/password credentials, updates TypeScript and Swift auth precedence, permits iOS local-network `ws://` inputs, and adds targeted tests plus pairing docs.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main rejects non-loopback plaintext iOS setup/deep-link hosts and setup-code generation emits only `bootstrapToken`. I did not establish a live iOS device reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the blockers are security design approval, draft state, and real iOS behavior proof rather than a narrow safe repair.

target.title: fix: allow iOS LAN gateway onboarding with shared auth
target.author: app/copilot-swe-agent
